copy.title: Jordan Smith leads Schwab Challenge. Microsoft crushes earnings. Bitcoin holds steady.
copy.keyTakeaway: Tech stocks split today: Microsoft jumped 5.4% while Nvidia and Tesla both slid. Golf's heating up with Jordan Smith leading the Charles Schwab Challenge. Entertainment's in flux—CBS is ditching Colbert for Byron Allen to pocket $55 million.

todaysHits.sports: Caitlin Clark's Valkyries beat the Fever. Details on WNBA action.
todaysHits.markets: Microsoft soars 5.4%, Nvidia and Tesla sink. SPY flat.
todaysHits.golf: Jordan Smith leads Charles Schwab at 10-under par.
todaysHits.culture: CBS dumps Colbert for Byron Allen in $55M deal.

lead.headline: Jordan Smith chasing first major at Schwab Challenge
lead.whatHappened: Jordan Smith is leading the Charles Schwab Challenge at 10-under with the tournament still in progress. He's got a real shot at a significant win if he holds.
lead.whyBullet1: Smith's been grinding for years on tour without a major breakthrough — this is the kind of moment that changes a career trajectory.
lead.whyBullet2: Mid-week tournaments like this get overshadowed by majors, but a win here puts real money and ranking points in his pocket heading into summer.
lead.whatToSay: If Smith closes this out, that's the kind of win that gets him in the conversation. If he doesn't, everyone remembers the one he let slip.

markets.mood: Microsoft's 5.4% pop carried the whole market today while chip stocks got hammered — classic sign that traders are rotating out of pure AI hype and into companies that actually make money from it.
markets.whyBullet1: If you own index funds, today reminded you that concentration risk is real: two or three mega-cap stocks can swing your entire portfolio even when the market barely moves.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch for earnings reactions Thursday and Friday — if software and cloud companies keep beating while chipmakers miss, that rotation accelerates and could reshape your tech allocation.
markets.bringUp: NVDA dropped 1.5% today while SPY only climbed 0.2%, meaning the chip sector's dead weight held back what should've been a stronger day for everyone else.

golf.headline: Jordan Smith leads Charles Schwab Challenge by one shot
golf.whyCare1: This is a FedEx Cup event—wins here matter for playoff positioning and points accumulation heading into the postseason.
golf.whyCare2: Thorbjornsen and Matsuyama are just one back, so the final rounds will feature serious names fighting for a prestigious win at a course that separates real ball strikers from pretenders.
golf.watchFor: Whether Jordan Smith can hold off the field down the stretch or if Thorbjornsen's youth and aggression catches him by Sunday.
golf.whatToSay: Smith's got a one-shot lead but Thorbjornsen's right there—this thing's going to come down to who doesn't flinch on the back nine.

culture.topic: Trump's State Fair bleeding talent fast
culture.whatHappened: Bret Michaels just pulled out of Trump's Great American State Fair, joining four other acts who've already bailed on the event.
culture.whyItMatters: When headliners start abandoning a high-profile gig, it signals real problems behind the scenes—whether that's money, logistics, or just not wanting the association.
culture.whatToSay: Even Bret Michaels doesn't want to touch that one.

culture.topic: Caitlin Clark's Valkyries close out the season
culture.whatHappened: Caitlin Clark and Sophie Cunningham led the Valkyries past the Fever, finishing out their season with a win.
culture.whyItMatters: Clark's rookie year is a wrap, and her impact on the WNBA's visibility has already changed what people watch—that matters for the league's long-term money and relevance.
culture.whatToSay: Clark made the WNBA appointment television. That doesn't happen by accident.

culture.whatHappened: Eddie Redmayne as a freelance assassin — methodical, charming, completely untraceable — until one investigator gets close.

copy.finalSharpTake: Microsoft's up five percent while the Magnificent Seven gets picked apart — that's the real story today. Nvidia and Tesla are bleeding, Apple's flat, but Satya Nadella's crew keeps climbing because the market finally believes in the AI upside without needing the stock to be a meme. Jordan Smith's sitting pretty at ten under in golf, which matters to exactly nobody unless you've got money on it. The broader play: rotation into quality earnings beats hype every single time.

glance.sports: Jordan Smith's at -10 and leading the Charles Schwab Challenge heading into the back nine.
glance.market: Tech's split today: MSFT crushed it at +5.4% while NVDA and TSLA both took L's, SPY barely moved at +0.2%.
glance.bestConvo: Why did Microsoft jump 5.4% while the Nasdaq basically flatlined? Something's different about the big bet on AI infrastructure right now.
glance.watchNext: Jordan Smith's back nine at Schwab — he's got a real chance to close this out clean. Also watch if MSFT's surge holds or if the market takes profits tomorrow.
glance.quickRec: If you're thinking about tech exposure, today showed you can't just buy the sector anymore. The winners and losers are way more separated than they used to be.
